name: csbdeep

metadata.name: csbdeep
metadata.summary: CSBDeep - a toolbox for Content-aware Image Restoration (CARE)

documentation:
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/csbdeep.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/csbdeep)
[![Anaconda-Server Badge](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/csbdeep/badges/version.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/csbdeep)
[![Test](https://github.com/CSBDeep/CSBDeep/workflows/Test/badge.svg)](https://github.com/CSBDeep/CSBDeep/actions?query=workflow%3ATest)
[![Test (PyPI)](https://github.com/CSBDeep/CSBDeep/workflows/Test%20(PyPI)/badge.svg)](https://github.com/CSBDeep/CSBDeep/actions?query=workflow%3A%22Test+%28PyPI%29%22)

# CSBDeep – a toolbox for CARE

This is the CSBDeep Python package, which provides a toolbox for content-aware restoration of fluorescence microscopy images (CARE), based on deep learning via Keras and TensorFlow.

Please see the documentation at http://csbdeep.bioimagecomputing.com/doc/.

documentation:
Save image in ImageJ-compatible TIFF format.

file

: File name

img

: Image

axes: str

: Axes of `img`

imsave_kwargs

: Keyword arguments for {func}`tifffile.imsave`

functions.name: csbdeep.io.load_training_data
documentation:
Load training data from file in `.npz` format.

The data file is expected to have the keys:

- `X` : Array of training input images.
- `Y` : Array of corresponding target images.
- `axes` : Axes of the training images.

# Parameters

file

: File name

validation_split

: Fraction of images to use as validation set during training.

axes: str, optional

: Must be provided in case the loaded data does not contain `axes` information.

n_images

: Can be used to limit the number of images loaded from data.

verbose

: Can be used to display information about the loaded images.

# Returns

tuple( tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray`), tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray`), str )

: Returns two tuples (`X_train`, `Y_train`), (`X_val`, `Y_val`) of training and validation sets
  and the axes of the input images.
  The tuple of validation data will be `None` if `validation_split = 0`.

functions.name: csbdeep.io.save_training_data
documentation:
Save training data in `.npz` format.

# Parameters

file

: File name

X

: Array of patches extracted from source images.

Y

: Array of corresponding target patches.

axes

: Axes of the extracted patches.

classes.name: csbdeep.models.care_upsampling.UpsamplingCARE

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_upsampling.UpsamplingCARE.predict
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image with low-resolution Z axis.

See {func}`CARE.predict` for documentation.

# Parameters

factor

: Upsampling factor for Z axis. It is important that this is chosen in correspondence
  to the subsampling factor used during training data generation.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_upsampling.UpsamplingCARE.predict_probabilistic
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image with low-resolution Z axis for probabilistic prediction.

See {func}`CARE.predict_probabilistic` for documentation.

# Parameters

factor

: Upsampling factor for Z axis. It is important that this is chosen in correspondence
  to the subsampling factor used during training data generation.

CARE network for combined image restoration and upsampling of one dimension.

Extends {class}`csbdeep.models.CARE` by replacing prediction
({func}`predict`, {func}`predict_probabilistic`) to first upsample Z before image restoration.

classes.name: csbdeep.models.care_isotropic.IsotropicCARE

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_isotropic.IsotropicCARE.predict
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image for isotropic reconstruction.

See {func}`CARE.predict` for documentation.

# Parameters

factor

: Upsampling factor for Z axis. It is important that this is chosen in correspondence
  to the subsampling factor used during training data generation.

batch_size

: Number of image slices that are processed together by the neural network.
  Reduce this value if out of memory errors occur.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_isotropic.IsotropicCARE.predict_probabilistic
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image to predict probability distribution for isotropic restored image.

See {func}`CARE.predict_probabilistic` for documentation.

# Parameters

factor

: Upsampling factor for Z axis. It is important that this is chosen in correspondence
  to the subsampling factor used during training data generation.

batch_size

: Number of image slices that are processed together by the neural network.
  Reduce this value if out of memory errors occur.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_isotropic.IsotropicCARE._predict_mean_and_scale
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image to restore isotropic resolution.

See {func}`predict` for parameter explanations.

# Returns

tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray` or None)

: If model is probabilistic, returns a tuple `(mean, scale)` that defines the parameters
  of per-pixel Laplace distributions. Otherwise, returns the restored image via a tuple `(restored,None)`

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_isotropic.IsotropicCARE._rotate
documentation: Rotate by 90 degrees around the first 2 axes.

CARE network for isotropic image reconstruction.

Extends {class}`csbdeep.models.CARE` by replacing prediction
({func}`predict`, {func}`predict_probabilistic`) to do isotropic reconstruction.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.base_model.BaseModel._config_class
documentation: Class of config to be used for this model.

Base model.

Subclasses must implement {func}`_build` and {func}`_config_class`.

# Parameters

config

: Valid configuration of a model (see {func}`BaseConfig.is_valid`).
  Will be saved to disk as JSON (`config.json`).
  If set to `None`, will be loaded from disk (must exist).

name

: Model name. Uses a timestamp if set to `None` (default).

basedir

: Directory that contains (or will contain) a folder with the given model name.
  Use `None` to disable saving (or loading) any data to (or from) disk (regardless of other parameters).

# Raises

FileNotFoundError

: If `config=None` and config cannot be loaded from disk.

ValueError

: Illegal arguments, including invalid configuration.

# Attributes

config

: Configuration of the model, as provided during instantiation.

keras_model

: Keras neural network model.

name

: Model name.

logdir

: Path to model folder (which stores configuration, weights, etc.)

methods.name: csbdeep.models.config.BaseConfig.update_loaded_config
documentation:
Called by model to update loaded config dictionary before config object is created

Can be used to modify or introduce/delete parameters, e.g. to ensure
backwards compatibility after new parameters have been introduced.

Parameters
----------
config : dict
    dictionary of config parameters loaded from file

Returns
-------
updated_config: dict

documentation: See class docstring.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.config.Config.is_valid
documentation:
Check if configuration is valid.

Returns
-------
bool
    Flag that indicates whether the current configuration values are valid.

Default configuration for a CARE model.

This configuration is meant to be used with {class}`CARE`
and related models (e.g., {class}`IsotropicCARE`).

# Parameters

axes

: Axes of the neural network (channel axis optional).

n_channel_in

: Number of channels of given input image.

n_channel_out

: Number of channels of predicted output image.

probabilistic

: Probabilistic prediction of per-pixel Laplace distributions or
  typical regression of per-pixel scalar values.

allow_new_parameters

: Allow adding new configuration attributes (i.e. not listed below).

kwargs

: Overwrite (or add) configuration attributes (see below).

# Example

```pycon
>>> config = Config('YX', probabilistic=True, unet_n_depth=3)
```

# Attributes

n_dim

: Dimensionality of input images (2 or 3).

unet_residual

: Parameter `residual` of {func}`csbdeep.nets.common_unet`. Default: `n_channel_in == n_channel_out`

unet_n_depth

: Parameter `n_depth` of {func}`csbdeep.nets.common_unet`. Default: `2`

unet_kern_size

: Parameter `kern_size` of {func}`csbdeep.nets.common_unet`. Default: `5 if n_dim==2 else 3`

unet_n_first

: Parameter `n_first` of {func}`csbdeep.nets.common_unet`. Default: `32`

unet_last_activation

: Parameter `last_activation` of {func}`csbdeep.nets.common_unet`. Default: `linear`

train_loss

: Name of training loss. Default: `'laplace' if probabilistic else 'mae'`

train_epochs

: Number of training epochs. Default: `100`

train_steps_per_epoch

: Number of parameter update steps per epoch. Default: `400`

train_learning_rate

: Learning rate for training. Default: `0.0004`

train_batch_size

: Batch size for training. Default: `16`

train_tensorboard

: Enable TensorBoard for monitoring training progress. Default: `True`

train_checkpoint

: Name of checkpoint file for model weights (only best are saved); set to `None` to disable. Default: `weights_best.h5`

train_reduce_lr

: Parameter {class}`dict` of [ReduceLROnPlateau] callback; set to `None` to disable. Default: `{'factor': 0.5, 'patience': 10, 'min_delta': 0}`

[reducelronplateau]: https://keras.io/callbacks/#reducelronplateau

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE.prepare_for_training
documentation:
Prepare for neural network training.

Calls {func}`csbdeep.internals.train.prepare_model` and creates
[Keras Callbacks](https://keras.io/callbacks/) to be used for training.

Note that this method will be implicitly called once by {func}`train`
(with default arguments) if not done so explicitly beforehand.

# Parameters

optimizer

: Instance of a [Keras Optimizer](https://keras.io/optimizers/) to be used for training.
  If `None` (default), uses `Adam` with the learning rate specified in `config`.

kwargs

: Additional arguments for {func}`csbdeep.internals.train.prepare_model`.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE.train
documentation:
Train the neural network with the given data.

# Parameters

X

: Array of source images.

Y

: Array of target images.

validation_data

: Tuple of arrays for source and target validation images.

epochs

: Optional argument to use instead of the value from `config`.

steps_per_epoch

: Optional argument to use instead of the value from `config`.

# Returns

`History` object

: See [Keras training history](https://keras.io/models/model/#fit).

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE.export_TF
documentation:
Export neural network via {func}`csbdeep.utils.tf.export_SavedModel`.

# Parameters

fname

: Path of the created SavedModel archive (will end with ".zip").
  If `None`, "\<model-directory>/TF_SavedModel.zip" will be used.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE.predict
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image to predict restored image.

# Parameters

img

: Raw input image

axes

: Axes of the input `img`.

normalizer

: Normalization of input image before prediction and (potentially) transformation back after prediction.

resizer

: If necessary, input image is resized to enable neural network prediction and result is (possibly)
  resized to yield original image size.

n_tiles

: Out of memory (OOM) errors can occur if the input image is too large.
  To avoid this problem, the input image is broken up into (overlapping) tiles
  that can then be processed independently and re-assembled to yield the restored image.
  This parameter denotes a tuple of the number of tiles for every image axis.
  Note that if the number of tiles is too low, it is adaptively increased until
  OOM errors are avoided, albeit at the expense of runtime.
  A value of `None` denotes that no tiling should initially be used.

# Returns

{class}`numpy.ndarray`

: Returns the restored image. If the model is probabilistic, this denotes the `mean` parameter of
  the predicted per-pixel Laplace distributions (i.e., the expected restored image).
  Axes semantics are the same as in the input image. Only if the output is multi-channel and
  the input image didn't have a channel axis, then output channels are appended at the end.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE.predict_probabilistic
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image to predict probability distribution for restored image.

See {func}`predict` for parameter explanations.

# Returns

{class}`csbdeep.internals.probability.ProbabilisticPrediction`

: Returns the probability distribution of the restored image.

# Raises

ValueError

: If this is not a probabilistic model.

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE._predict_mean_and_scale
documentation:
Apply neural network to raw image to predict restored image.

See {func}`predict` for parameter explanations.

# Returns

tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray` or None)

: If model is probabilistic, returns a tuple `(mean, scale)` that defines the parameters
  of per-pixel Laplace distributions. Otherwise, returns the restored image via a tuple `(restored,None)`

methods.name: csbdeep.models.care_standard.CARE._axes_div_by
documentation: def _limit_tiling(self,img_shape,n_tiles,block_sizes):
img_shape, n_tiles, block_sizes = np.array(img_shape), np.array(n_tiles), np.array(block_sizes)
n_tiles_limit = np.ceil(img_shape / block_sizes) # each tile must be at least one block in size
return [int(t) for t in np.minimum(n_tiles,n_tiles_limit)]

Standard CARE network for image restoration and enhancement.

Uses a convolutional neural network created by {func}`csbdeep.internals.nets.common_unet`.
Note that isotropic reconstruction and manifold extraction/projection are not supported here
(see {class}`csbdeep.models.IsotropicCARE` ).

# Parameters

config

: Valid configuration of CARE network (see {func}`Config.is_valid`).
  Will be saved to disk as JSON (`config.json`).
  If set to `None`, will be loaded from disk (must exist).

name

: Model name. Uses a timestamp if set to `None` (default).

basedir

: Directory that contains (or will contain) a folder with the given model name.
  Use `None` to disable saving (or loading) any data to (or from) disk (regardless of other parameters).

# Raises

FileNotFoundError

: If `config=None` and config cannot be loaded from disk.

ValueError

: Illegal arguments, including invalid configuration.

# Example

```pycon
>>> model = CARE(config, 'my_model')
```

# Attributes

config

: Configuration of CARE network, as provided during instantiation.

keras_model

: Keras neural network model.

name

: Model name.

logdir

: Path to model folder (which stores configuration, weights, etc.)

modules.name: csbdeep.data.rawdata
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: csbdeep.data.rawdata.RawData

methods.name: csbdeep.data.rawdata.RawData.from_folder
documentation:
Get pairs of corresponding TIFF images read from folders.

Two images correspond to each other if they have the same file name, but are located in different folders.

# Parameters

basepath

: Base folder that contains sub-folders with images.

source_dirs

: List of folder names relative to `basepath` that contain the source images (e.g., with low SNR).

target_dir

: Folder name relative to `basepath` that contains the target images (e.g., with high SNR).

axes

: Semantics of axes of loaded images (assumed to be the same for all images).

pattern

: Glob-style pattern to match the desired TIFF images.

# Returns

RawData

: {obj}`RawData` object, whose `generator` is used to yield all matching TIFF pairs.
  The generator will return a tuple `(x,y,axes,mask)`, where `x` is from
  `source_dirs` and `y` is the corresponding image from the `target_dir`;
  `mask` is set to `None`.

# Raises

FileNotFoundError

: If an image found in a `source_dir` does not exist in `target_dir`.

# Example

```pycon
>>> !tree data
data
├── GT
│   ├── imageA.tif
│   ├── imageB.tif
│   └── imageC.tif
├── source1
│   ├── imageA.tif
│   └── imageB.tif
└── source2
    ├── imageA.tif
    └── imageC.tif
```

```pycon
>>> data = RawData.from_folder(basepath='data', source_dirs=['source1','source2'], target_dir='GT', axes='YX')
>>> n_images = data.size
>>> for source_x, target_y, axes, mask in data.generator():
...     pass
```

methods.name: csbdeep.data.rawdata.RawData.from_arrays
documentation: Get pairs of corresponding images from numpy arrays.

{func}`collections.namedtuple` with three fields: `generator`, `size`, and `description`.

# Parameters

generator

: Function without arguments that returns a generator that yields tuples `(x,y,axes,mask)`,
  where `x` is a source image (e.g., with low SNR) with `y` being the corresponding target image
  (e.g., with high SNR); `mask` can either be `None` or a boolean array that denotes which
  pixels are eligible to extracted in {func}`create_patches`. Note that `x`, `y`, and `mask`
  must all be of type {class}`numpy.ndarray` and are assumed to have the same shape, where the
  string `axes` indicates the order and presence of axes of all three arrays.

size

: Number of tuples that the `generator` will yield.

description

: Textual description of the raw data.

modules.name: csbdeep.data.transform
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: csbdeep.data.transform.anisotropic_distortions
documentation:
Simulate anisotropic distortions.

Modify the first image (obtained from input generator) along one axis to mimic the
distortions that typically occur due to low resolution along the Z axis.
Note that the modified image is finally upscaled to obtain the same resolution
as the unmodified input image and is yielded as the 'source' image (see {class}`RawData`).
The mask from the input generator is simply passed through.

The following operations are applied to the image (in order):

1. Convolution with PSF
2. Poisson noise
3. Gaussian noise
4. Subsampling along `subsample_axis`
5. Upsampling along `subsample_axis` (to former size).

# Parameters

subsample

: Subsampling factor to mimic distortions along Z.

psf

: Point spread function (PSF) that is supposed to mimic blurring
  of the microscope due to reduced axial resolution. Set to `None` to disable.

psf_axes

: Axes of the PSF. If `None`, psf axes are assumed to be the same as of the image
  that it is applied to.

poisson_noise

: Flag to indicate whether Poisson noise should be applied to the image.

gauss_sigma

: Standard deviation of white Gaussian noise to be added to the image.

subsample_axis

: Subsampling image axis (default X).

yield_target

: Which image from the input generator should be yielded by the generator ('source' or 'target').
  If 'source', the unmodified input/source image (from which the distorted image is computed)
  is yielded as the target image. If 'target', the target image from the input generator is simply
  passed through.

crop_threshold

: The subsample factor must evenly divide the image size along the subsampling axis to prevent
  potential image misalignment. If this is not the case the subsample factors are
  modified and the raw image may be cropped along the subsampling axis
  up to a fraction indicated by `crop_threshold`.

# Returns

Transform

: Returns a {class}`Transform` object intended to be used with {func}`create_patches`.

# Raises

ValueError

: Various reasons.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.transform.permute_axes
documentation:
Transformation to permute images axes.

# Parameters

axes

: Target axes, to which the input images will be permuted.

# Returns

Transform

: Returns a {class}`Transform` object whose `generator` will
  perform the axes permutation of `x`, `y`, and `mask`.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.transform.crop_images
documentation:
Transformation to crop all images (and mask).

Note that slices must be compatible with the image size.

# Parameters

slices

: List of slices to apply to each dimension of the image.

# Returns

Transform

: Returns a {class}`Transform` object whose `generator` will
  perform image cropping of `x`, `y`, and `mask`.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.transform.broadcast_target
documentation:
Transformation to broadcast the target image to the shape of the source image.

# Parameters

target_axes

: Axes of the target image before broadcasting.
  If `None`, assumed to be the same as for the source image.

# Returns

Transform

: Returns a {class}`Transform` object whose `generator` will
  perform broadcasting of `y` to match the shape of `x`.

classes.name: csbdeep.data.transform.Transform

methods.name: csbdeep.data.transform.Transform.identity
documentation:
Returns
-------
Transform
    Identity transformation that passes every input through unchanged.

Extension of {func}`collections.namedtuple` with three fields: `name`, `generator`, and `size`.

# Parameters

name

: Name of the applied transformation.

generator

: Function that takes a generator as input and itself returns a generator; input and returned
  generator have the same structure as that of {class}`RawData`.
  The purpose of the returned generator is to augment the images provided by the input generator
  through additional transformations.
  It is important that the returned generator also includes every input tuple unchanged.

size

: Number of transformations applied to every image (obtained from the input generator).

modules.name: csbdeep.data.generate
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.no_background_patches
documentation:
Returns a patch filter to be used by {func}`create_patches` to determine for each image pair which patches
are eligible for sampling. The purpose is to only sample patches from "interesting" regions of the raw image that
actually contain a substantial amount of non-background signal. To that end, a maximum filter is applied to the target image
to find the largest values in a region.

# Parameters

threshold

: Scalar threshold between 0 and 1 that will be multiplied with the (outlier-robust)
  maximum of the image (see `percentile` below) to denote a lower bound.
  Only patches with a maximum value above this lower bound are eligible to be sampled.

percentile

: Percentile value to denote the (outlier-robust) maximum of an image, i.e. should be close 100.

# Returns

function

: Function that takes an image pair `(y,x)` and the patch size as arguments and
  returns a binary mask of the same size as the image (to denote the locations
  eligible for sampling for {func}`create_patches`). At least one pixel of the
  binary mask must be `True`, otherwise there are no patches to sample.

# Raises

ValueError

: Illegal arguments.
# Patch filter

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.sample_patches_from_multiple_stacks
documentation: sample matching patches of size `patch_size` from all arrays in `datas`
# Sample patches

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate._valid_low_high_percentiles
documentation: # Create training data

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.sample_percentiles
documentation:
Sample percentile values from a uniform distribution.

Parameters
----------
pmin : tuple
    Tuple of two values that denotes the interval for sampling low percentiles.
pmax : tuple
    Tuple of two values that denotes the interval for sampling high percentiles.

Returns
-------
function
    Function without arguments that returns `(pl,ph)`, where `pl` (`ph`) is a sampled low (high) percentile.

Raises
------
ValueError
    Illegal arguments.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.norm_percentiles
documentation:
Normalize extracted patches based on percentiles from corresponding raw image.

# Parameters

percentiles

: A tuple (`pmin`, `pmax`) or a function that returns such a tuple, where the extracted patches
  are (affinely) normalized in such that a value of 0 (1) corresponds to the `pmin`-th (`pmax`-th) percentile
  of the raw image (default: {func}`sample_percentiles`).

relu_last

: Flag to indicate whether the last activation of the CARE network is/will be using
  a ReLU activation function (default: `False`)

# Return

function

: Function that does percentile-based normalization to be used in {func}`create_patches`.

# Raises

ValueError

: Illegal arguments.

# Todo

`relu_last` flag problematic/inelegant.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.create_patches
documentation:
Create normalized training data to be used for neural network training.

# Parameters

raw_data

: Object that yields matching pairs of raw images.

patch_size

: Shape of the patches to be extraced from raw images.
  Must be compatible with the number of dimensions and axes of the raw images.
  As a general rule, use a power of two along all XYZT axes, or at least divisible by 8.

n_patches_per_image

: Number of patches to be sampled/extracted from each raw image pair (after transformations, see below).

patch_axes

: Axes of the extracted patches. If `None`, will assume to be equal to that of transformed raw data.

save_file

: File name to save training data to disk in `.npz` format (see {func}`csbdeep.io.save_training_data`).
  If `None`, data will not be saved.

transforms

: List of {class}`Transform` objects that apply additional transformations to the raw images.
  This can be used to augment the set of raw images (e.g., by including rotations).
  Set to `None` to disable. Default: `None`.

patch_filter

: Function to determine for each image pair which patches are eligible to be extracted
  (default: {func}`no_background_patches`). Set to `None` to disable.

normalization

: Function that takes arguments `(patches_x, patches_y, x, y, mask, channel)`, whose purpose is to
  normalize the patches (`patches_x`, `patches_y`) extracted from the associated raw images
  (`x`, `y`, with `mask`; see {class}`RawData`). Default: {func}`norm_percentiles`.

shuffle

: Randomly shuffle all extracted patches.

verbose

: Display overview of images, transforms, etc.

# Returns

tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray`, str)

: Returns a tuple (`X`, `Y`, `axes`) with the normalized extracted patches from all (transformed) raw images
  and their axes.
  `X` is the array of patches extracted from source images with `Y` being the array of corresponding target patches.
  The shape of `X` and `Y` is as follows: `(n_total_patches, n_channels, ...)`.
  For single-channel images, `n_channels` will be 1.

# Raises

ValueError

: Various reasons.

# Example

```pycon
>>> raw_data = RawData.from_folder(basepath='data', source_dirs=['source1','source2'], target_dir='GT', axes='ZYX')
>>> X, Y, XY_axes = create_patches(raw_data, patch_size=(32,128,128), n_patches_per_image=16)
```

# Todo

- Save created patches directly to disk using {class}`numpy.memmap` or similar?
  Would allow to work with large data that doesn't fit in memory.

functions.name: csbdeep.data.generate.create_patches_reduced_target
documentation:
Create normalized training data to be used for neural network training.

In contrast to {func}`create_patches`, it is assumed that the target image has reduced
dimensionality (i.e. size 1) along one or several axes (`reduction_axes`).

# Parameters

raw_data

: See {func}`create_patches`.

patch_size

: See {func}`create_patches`.

n_patches_per_image

: See {func}`create_patches`.

reduction_axes

: Axes where the target images have a reduced dimension (i.e. size 1) compared to the source images.

target_axes

: Axes of the raw target images. If `None`, will be assumed to be equal to that of the raw source images.

kwargs

: Additional parameters as in {func}`create_patches`.

# Returns

tuple({class}`numpy.ndarray`, {class}`numpy.ndarray`, str)

: See {func}`create_patches`. Note that the shape of the target data will be 1 along all reduction axes.

functions.name: csbdeep.internals.nets.common_unet
documentation:
Construct a common CARE neural net based on U-Net [^footnote-1] and residual learning [^footnote-2] to be used for image restoration/enhancement.

# Parameters

n_dim

: number of image dimensions (2 or 3)

n_depth

: number of resolution levels of U-Net architecture

kern_size

: size of convolution filter in all image dimensions

n_first

: number of convolution filters for first U-Net resolution level (value is doubled after each downsampling operation)

n_channel_out

: number of channels of the predicted output image

residual

: if True, model will internally predict the residual w.r.t. the input (typically better)
  requires number of input and output image channels to be equal

prob_out

: standard regression (False) or probabilistic prediction (True)
  if True, model will predict two values for each input pixel (mean and positive scale value)

last_activation

: name of activation function for the final output layer

# Returns

function

: Function to construct the network, which takes as argument the shape of the input image

# Example

```pycon
>>> model = common_unet(2, 1,3,16, 1, True, False)(input_shape)
```

# References

[^footnote-1]: Olaf Ronneberger, Philipp Fischer, Thomas Brox, *U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation*, MICCAI 2015

[^footnote-2]: Kaiming He, Xiangyu Zhang, Shaoqing Ren, Jian Sun. *Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition*, CVPR 2016

functions.name: csbdeep.internals.nets.common_unet_by_name
documentation:
Shorthand notation for equivalent use of {func}`common_unet`.

# Parameters

model

: define model to be created via string, which is parsed as a regular expression:
  `^(?P<model>resunet|unet)(?P<n_dim>\d)(?P<prob_out>p)?_(?P<n_depth>\d+)_(?P<kern_size>\d+)_(?P<n_first>\d+)(_(?P<n_channel_out>\d+)out)?(_(?P<last_activation>.+)-last)?$`

# Returns

function

: Calls {func}`common_unet` with the respective parameters.

# Raises

ValueError

: If argument `model` is not a valid string according to the regular expression.

# Example

```pycon
>>> model = common_unet_by_name('resunet2_1_3_16_1out')(input_shape)
>>> # equivalent to: model = common_unet(2, 1,3,16, 1, True, False)(input_shape)
```

# Todo

Backslashes in docstring for regexp not rendered correctly.

classes.name: csbdeep.internals.train.RollingSequence
documentation:
Helper class for creating batches for rolling sequence.

Create batches of size `batch_size` that contain indices in `range(data_size)`.
To that end, the data indices are repeated (rolling), either in ascending order or
shuffled if `shuffle=True`. If taking batches sequentially, all data indices will
appear equally often. All calls to `batch(i)` will return the same batch for same i.
Parameter `length` will only determine the result of `len`, it has no effect otherwise.
Note that batch_size is allowed to be larger than data_size.

functions.name: csbdeep.internals.predict.tile_iterator_1d
documentation:
Tile iterator for one dimension of array x.

Parameters
----------
x : numpy.ndarray
    Input array
axis : int
    Axis which sould be tiled, all other axis not tiled
n_tiles : int
    Targeted number of tiles for axis of x (see guarantee below)
block_size : int
    Axis of x is assumed to be evenly divisible by block_size
    All tiles are aligned with the block_size
n_block_overlap : int
    Tiles will overlap at least this many blocks (see guarantee below)
guarantee : str
    Can be either 'size' or 'n_tiles':
    'size':    The size of all tiles is guaranteed to be the same,
               but the number of tiles can be different and the
               amount of overlap can be larger than requested.
    'n_tiles': The size of tiles can be different at the beginning and end,
               but the number of tiles is guarantee to be the one requested.
               The mount of overlap is also exactly as requested.

functions.name: csbdeep.internals.predict.tile_iterator
documentation:
Tile iterator for n-d arrays.

Yields block-aligned tiles (`block_sizes`) that have at least
a certain amount of overlapping blocks (`n_block_overlaps`)
with their neighbors. Also yields slices that allow to map each
tile back to the original array x.

# Notes

- Tiles will not go beyond the array boundary (i.e. no padding).
  This means the shape of x must be evenly divisible by the respective block_size.
- It is not guaranteed that all tiles have the same size if guarantee is not 'size'.

# Parameters

x

: Input array.

n_tiles

: Number of tiles for each dimension of x.

block_sizes

: Block sizes for each dimension of x.
  The shape of x is assumed to be evenly divisible by block_sizes.
  All tiles are aligned with block_sizes.

n_block_overlaps

: Tiles will at least overlap this many blocks in each dimension.

guarantee

: Can be either 'size' or 'n_tiles':
  'size': The size of all tiles is guaranteed to be the same,

  > but the number of tiles can be different and the
  > amount of overlap can be larger than requested.

  'n_tiles': The size of tiles can be different at the beginning and end,

  : but the number of tiles is guarantee to be the one requested.
    The mount of overlap is also exactly as requested.

# Example

Duplicate an array tile-by-tile:

```pycon
>>> x = np.array(...)
>>> y = np.empty_like(x)
>>>
>>> for tile,s_src,s_dst in tile_iterator(x, n_tiles, block_sizes, n_block_overlaps):
>>>     y[s_dst] = tile[s_src]
>>>
>>> np.allclose(x,y)
True
```

functions.name: csbdeep.utils.tf.limit_gpu_memory
documentation:
Limit GPU memory allocation for TensorFlow (TF) backend.

# Parameters

fraction

: Limit TF to use only a fraction (value between 0 and 1) of the available GPU memory.
  Reduced memory allocation can be disabled if fraction is set to `None`.

allow_growth

: If `False` (default), TF will allocate all designated (see `fraction`) memory all at once.
  If `True`, TF will allocate memory as needed up to the limit imposed by `fraction`; this may
  incur a performance penalty due to memory fragmentation.

total_memory

: Total amount of available GPU memory (in MB).

# Raises

ValueError

: If `fraction` is not `None` or a float value between 0 and 1.

NotImplementedError

: If TensorFlow is not used as the backend.

functions.name: csbdeep.utils.tf.export_SavedModel
documentation:
Export Keras model in TensorFlow's [SavedModel] format.

See [Your Model in Fiji] to learn how to use the exported model with our CSBDeep Fiji plugins.

# Parameters

model

: Keras model to be exported.

outpath

: Path of the file/folder that the model will exported to.

meta

: Metadata to be saved in an additional `meta.json` file.

format

: Can be 'dir' to export as a directory or 'zip' (default) to export as a ZIP file.

# Raises

ValueError

: Illegal arguments.

[savedmodel]: https://www.tensorflow.org/programmers_guide/saved_model#structure_of_a_savedmodel_directory
[your model in fiji]: https://github.com/CSBDeep/CSBDeep_website/wiki/Your-Model-in-Fiji
